content.thinking: Let me start by exploring the repository structure to understand the codebase.
Let me search for the negated flag handling code.

Now I can see the issue clearly. In `src/_parser.ts`, the "Apply negated flags" section just sets `(out as any)[name] = false` for the literal flag name from `--no-flag`. But it doesn't propagate to aliases or the main option.

Then the "Propagate values between aliases" section uses `undefined` checks, so it only propagates when the target is `undefined`. Since defaults are already set, this won't propagate negated values.

The fix needs to:
1. When a negated flag is encountered, also set the main option and all its aliases to `false`
2. This needs to happen before the alias propagation step


3. I need to look at the actual code structure to understand how the aliases are tracked and where to make this change.

content.text: The fix is clear. When applying negated flags, we need to also propagate `false` to the main option (if the negated name is an alias) and to all aliases (if the negated name is the main option).

content.content: The file /private/var/folders/3g/qls22cy50f14fz2tbcpbwy7h0000gp/T/bench-swe-822991944/repo/src/_parser.ts has been updated successfully.

lines: +  // Apply negated flags and propagate to main option and all aliases
+    // If name is an alias, also set the main option
+    // Set all aliases of either this name or its main option to false

content.text:
All 54 tests pass. The fix correctly propagates `--no-` negation bidirectionally:

**What changed** in `src/_parser.ts` (lines 145-159):

When a `--no-flag` argument is encountered, after setting `flag=false`, the fix now:
1. Looks up if `flag` is an alias — if so, sets the main option to `false` too
2. Looks up all aliases of either the flag or its main option, and sets each to `false`

This happens before the general alias propagation loop (which only fills `undefined` values), so negated flags correctly override defaults on both the main option and its aliases.

result:
All 54 tests pass. The fix correctly propagates `--no-` negation bidirectionally:

**What changed** in `src/_parser.ts` (lines 145-159):

When a `--no-flag` argument is encountered, after setting `flag=false`, the fix now:
1. Looks up if `flag` is an alias — if so, sets the main option to `false` too
2. Looks up all aliases of either the flag or its main option, and sets each to `false`

This happens before the general alias propagation loop (which only fills `undefined` values), so negated flags correctly override defaults on both the main option and its aliases.
